task: BUILD-PLAN v3 trinn 4B-3 - minimum contract for flow-step (Scorecard) + disabled-entry (Landing)
classification_rationale: Touches the shared Tee Off round wizard (app-shell flow) and the app-wide disabled landing gate. Per cross-cutting rule B this defaults to product-wide.
risk_rationale: No production code changes. Adds two deterministic UI contract tests only (flow-step reachability + disabled-entry negative). Worst realistic case is a failing test.
changed_files_note: No app.js / styles.css / other production file is changed. CONTRACT-REGISTER.md is listed LAST and is only updated after the local tests are actually green - not written in this step. No new fixture: the existing seedEmptyState (default profile + seeded SEED_COURSES) is sufficient; no Supabase, no production data, no writes.
activation_policy: Real, visible user journey only. Scorecard: click the Tee Off nav button (role+name), click the visible Golf Round kind card (its own handler advances the wizard to step 1), click Next in the visible step 1, choose the first valid non-empty course via the real search widget (#r-course-search + suggestion click), click Next in the visible step 2. Landing: observe the booted DOM after the last landing-gate timer. Real visible interactions only - no inline click-attribute selectors and no internal tab/render/welcome-wizard functions.
determinism: seedEmptyState for all; blocked service workers. Seeded SEED_COURSES make the course search deterministic. No production data, no network, no Supabase writes.

scorecard_contract.nature: dynamic render inside Tee Off (step 3 of the in-page wizard, toggled via style.display) - NOT a tab, hidden subpage or modal
covers: body[data-tab] = round and Tee Off nav button active
at least one actual score surface visible (which one is not locked)
explicitly_not_covered: alternative score modes (winner, total, nassau) and resume-active-round entry
scorecard_contract.baseline_statement: Today's Scorecard design is NOT an approved baseline. This contract covers only flow, active state and basic mounting. A future redesign may replace the internal DOM and update this contract without it being treated as a regression.

landing_contract.source_state: No static #tab-landing exists. The landing/welcome overlay #wl-overlay is injected only by _showWelcomeWizard(), which frLandingGate() (app.js:2529-2545) never calls because `const show = false` (app.js:2533). Any existing #wl-overlay is removed. The gate re-runs on load + timers at 700/1600/3500 ms, so the disabled state is stable.
covers: #wl-overlay not mounted (count 0) after the last (3500 ms) gate
body does not carry the wl-showing class
no landing control in the main navigation (nav#tabs button[data-tab=landing] count 0)
Clubhouse (#tab-dashboard) is still the active tab - landing did not take over
landing_contract.method_note: Disabled status is proven only by observing the booted DOM. The test never calls _showWelcomeWizard or window.activateTab.

expected_test_count.note: 1 Scorecard flow test + 1 Landing disabled-entry test, x4 projects (chromium-390, chromium-412, webkit-390, webkit-412).

product_findings.finding: Step 1 and step 2 each render a button with the same accessible name ("Next ->"). Role + accessible name alone is ambiguous across the two steps.
product_findings.decision: Contract scopes the click to the visible wizard step (page.locator('#rwz-step-1'|'#rwz-step-2').getByRole('button', {name:/Next/})). No app change; scoping is by container id, not onclick. A future a11y pass could give distinct names.

product_findings.finding: The landing/welcome experience is hard-disabled via `const show = false` (app.js:2533). _showWelcomeWizard is latent code with no UI entry.
product_findings.decision: Registered, not repaired. Contracted only as a disabled-entry (negative). Re-enabling is a separate product decision.

product_findings.finding: A round in progress jumps straight to the scorecard: activateTab-time code (app.js:3635-3637) calls rwzNext(3) + rwzBuildScorecard, bypassing steps 1-2.
product_findings.decision: Out of scope for the 4B-3 fresh representative flow. Belongs to the trinn 5 state matrix / 4C. Not contracted now.

product_findings.finding: The "Course" control is not a native combobox. #r-course is a hidden <select style=display:none> (Golf Dashboard.html:560). The real user control is the text field #r-course-search plus a suggestion list of .course-suggestion-item[data-id] (div, mousedown handler, no role). rwzNext(2->3) only requires a non-empty #r-course value.
product_findings.decision: Contract drives the real widget (type a seeded course name, click the first suggestion). Registered as a11y/structure finding; no app change in 4B-3. A future pass could expose a properly-roled course control.

product_findings.finding: Tapping a .teeoff-card is the live way forward: the card's own handler (app.js:17173-17178) calls showRoundForm(kind), which reveals #r-form-golf (wizard step 1) and hides #r-kind-panel - including the CONTINUE button (#teeoff-continue) that lives inside it. CONTINUE is therefore NOT part of the live journey; it is only a fallback for the default selection when no card is tapped. Runtime confirmed: after opening Tee Off and clicking Golf Round, #rwz-step-1 becomes visible and CONTINUE is not present.
product_findings.decision: The authoritative journey clicks the visible Golf Round card (role + accessible name) and expects #rwz-step-1 to become visible. The contract has NO dependency on CONTINUE. Test and manifest describe the same journey. No app change in 4B-3.

product_findings.finding: The PWA install banner (#pwa-install-banner, role=dialog, aria-label 'Install Fairway Rivalry') can appear asynchronously: showInstallBanner() adds the .show class ~2200 ms after load on mobile viewports (app.js:15146, gated by isMobileDevice()) and ~1500 ms after beforeinstallprompt (app.js:15132). In WebKit 390/412 it becomes visible mid-journey and, as a bottom overlay, intercepts pointer events - blocking real inputs (Next in step 1 on WebKit-390, Next in step 2 on WebKit-412). Chromium 390/412 did not surface it. seedEmptyState clears the session dismissed flag, so the banner is not pre-suppressed.
product_findings.decision: Stabilization only - NOT part of the Scorecard product contract. The test dismisses the banner through its existing visible user action: clicking the 'Later' button (#pwa-install-dismiss, role=button, accessible name 'Later'), which calls dismissInstallPrompt() -> removes .show and sets a session flag so it does not reappear. Guarded before the Next clicks the banner was observed to block, and retried once if it slips in during a click. No force, no internal function calls, no DOM/CSS/storage manipulation, no hidden selectors.

flow_status.Scorecard (#rwz-step-3): PARTIAL - flow + active state + basic mounting contracted; design not an approved baseline; winner/total/nassau + resume-active-round uncovered

not_in_scope: alternative score modes (winner/total/nassau) and resume-active-round entry
repairing findings T, U, V, W, X
